questions: Is the 'compressed button below the video' you mentioned the Maximize/Minimize timeline button in the full editor's divider bar (between preview and timeline), or a different UI element? This affects whether additional CompactPreview changes are needed.

Should the video editor compress when the generations pane is hovered (temporarily open) as well as when locked, or only when locked? Currently the compact editor pane compresses for both — matching that behavior is the default plan.
Are there other panes (shots, tasks) that should also affect the video editor's available space when locked, or is this only about the bottom generations pane?
success_criteria: Locking the generations pane while on `/tools/video-editor?timeline=...` causes the full editor to shrink vertically, with the timeline and preview compressing to fit above the generations pane.
Unlocking the generations pane restores the editor to full viewport height.
The compression/expansion transition is smooth (300ms ease-smooth).
The compact editor pane (top) continues to work correctly — no regression.
No z-index or overlap issues between the video editor and the generations pane.
assumptions: The bug is about the full-screen video editor page (`/tools/video-editor?timeline=xxx`), not the compact editor pane. The compact pane already compresses via EditorPaneTab's generationsOffset subtraction.
The 'compressed button below the video' refers to a known UI reference behavior where the editor content shrinks to minimum space — likely the compact pane's behavior or the timeline maximize button.
Only the bottom generations pane needs to affect the video editor height. Side panes (shots, tasks) already don't render their margin offsets when `isVideoEditorShellActive`.
`effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight` (from PanesContext) is the correct value to subtract, consistent with how other layout code uses it.
The `h-screen` → `h-full` change in VideoEditorPage.tsx won't affect non-editor routes because that component only renders on the video editor route.
